privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic drone wing rib in LibreCAD from hand-selected airfoil coordinates. The coordinate file (naca0012_200mm.txt) contains 29 symmetric points scaled to a 200mm chord with 18% maximum thickness. Despite the filename and description claiming NACA 0012 airfoil data, the actual coordinates do NOT match NACA 0012 specifications (which have 12% thickness); instead they are simplified hand-picked points with non-standard thickness. Three circular holes are placed on a separate CUTOUTS layer: Main Spar at (50,0) with 10mm diameter, Rear Spar at (140,0) with 6mm diameter, and Lightening Hole at (95,0) with 24mm diameter. The verifier checks for file creation, valid DXF format, correct layer names, airfoil geometry presence/closure, and hole specifications with 1mm position and 0.5mm radius tolerances.
